a.en: used before a singular noun to refer to one unspecified thing

ex.en: I have a cat.
Tell me about your trip.

about.en: used to show that a number or amount is not exact

ex.en: It's about five o'clock.

above.en: in or to a higher position than something

ex.en: The plane flew above the clouds.

above.en: mentioned or written earlier in a document

ex.en: Please see the above example.

action.en: the process of doing something, especially to achieve an aim

ex.en: We need to take action now.

activity.en: something that you do for pleasure, exercise, or as part of your life

ex.en: Swimming is a good activity for kids.

actor.en: a person who performs in a play, movie, or television show

ex.en: He is a famous actor in Korea.

add.en: put numbers or amounts together to get a total

ex.en: Add two and three to get five.

add.en: put something with another thing to make it bigger or more complete

ex.en: She added sugar to her coffee.

address.en: the number of the building and the name of the street and town where someone lives or works

ex.en: What's your home address?

address.en: give a formal speech to a group of people

ex.en: The president will address the nation tonight.
ex.ko: 대통령은 오늘 밤 국민에게 연설할 것이다.

address.en: deal with or discuss a problem or issue

ex.en: We need to address this issue immediately.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 문제를 즉시 다뤄야 한다.

afraid.en: feeling fear because you think something bad might happen

ex.en: She is afraid of spiders.

afraid.en: used to politely say you are sorry about something

ex.en: I'm afraid I can't come to the party.
ex.ko: 죄송하지만 저는 파티에 못 갈 것 같아요.

after.en: later than a particular time or event

ex.en: We'll meet after lunch.

afternoon.en: the part of the day between noon and evening

ex.en: I have a meeting this afternoon.
Can you say that again?

age.en: the number of years someone has lived

ex.en: What is your age?
We live in the digital age.

ago.en: before the present time; in the past

ex.en: She left two hours ago.

agree.en: have the same opinion as someone else, or accept a suggestion

ex.en: I agree with your plan.

airplane.en: a vehicle with wings and engines that flies in the air

ex.en: We took an airplane to New York.

airport.en: a place where airplanes take off and land

ex.en: My flight leaves from the airport at noon.

album.en: a collection of songs released together by a singer or band

ex.en: She released a new album last week.

album.en: a book with blank pages used for keeping photographs

ex.en: We looked through an old photo album.

all.en: the whole amount or number of something

ex.en: All the students passed the test.

all right.en: satisfactory, acceptable, or in good condition

ex.en: Are you all right?
It's almost midnight.
She lives alone in a small apartment.

along.en: moving or existing beside or through the length of something

ex.en: We walked along the beach.

already.en: before now or before a particular time; earlier than expected

ex.en: She has already finished her homework.
He plays soccer and also basketball.
She always arrives on time.
I am a student.

an.en: used before a singular noun starting with a vowel sound to refer to one unspecified thing

ex.en: She ate an apple.

and.en: used to connect words, phrases, or sentences

ex.en: I like tea and coffee.
He was angry about the delay.

animal.en: a living creature that is not a plant, such as a dog, bird, or fish

ex.en: Lions are wild animals.

another.cue: 이미 있는 것 외에 하나 더
another.en: one more, or a different one, of the same kind

ex.en: Can I have another cup of coffee?
ex.ko: 커피 한 잔 더 마셔도 될까요?

answer.en: something said or written to react to a question

ex.en: I don't know the answer to this question.

answer.en: say or write something in response to a question

ex.en: She answered all my questions.

any.en: used to refer to one, some, or any amount of a thing, especially in questions or negatives

ex.en: Do you have any questions?

anybody.en: any person; used mainly in questions and negative sentences

ex.en: Is anybody home?

anyone.en: any person; used mainly in questions and negative sentences

ex.en: Anyone can join the club.
ex.ko: 누구나 그 동아리에 가입할 수 있다.

anything.en: any thing; used mainly in questions and negative sentences

ex.en: I don't want anything to eat.

apple.en: a round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin and white flesh

ex.en: She ate a red apple.

apron.en: a piece of clothing worn over the front of your clothes to keep them clean, especially while cooking

ex.en: She put on an apron before cooking.

are.en: the form of 'be' used with 'you', 'we', or 'they'

ex.en: You are my best friend.

arm.en: the part of the body from the shoulder to the hand

ex.en: He broke his arm while skiing.
The soldiers were armed with rifles.

around.en: on all sides of something, or in a circle surrounding it

ex.en: The children sat around the table.

around.en: used to show that a number or time is not exact

ex.en: It costs around ten dollars.
The train arrived on time.

art.en: the creation of paintings, drawings, or other works that express ideas or beauty

ex.en: She studies art at university.

article.en: a piece of writing on a particular topic in a newspaper, magazine, or website

ex.en: I read an interesting article about space.
ex.ko: 나는 우주에 관한 흥미로운 기사를 읽었다.

article.cue: a, an, the 같은 문법 요소
article.en: a word such as 'a', 'an', or 'the' used before a noun

ex.en: 'The' is a definite article.

as.en: used to say what role or purpose someone or something has

ex.en: She works as a teacher.

as.en: used to compare two things that are equal or similar

ex.en: He is as tall as his father.
As it was raining, we stayed inside.
ex.ko: 비가 왔기 때문에 우리는 실내에 있었다.

ask.en: say something in order to get information

ex.en: Can I ask you a question?

ask.en: request someone to do or give something

ex.en: She asked me to help her.

at.en: used to show a specific place or time

ex.en: I'll meet you at the station at six.

aunt.en: the sister of your mother or father, or the wife of your uncle

ex.en: My aunt lives in Busan.

autumn.en: the season between summer and winter

ex.en: The leaves turn red in autumn.
The baby was awake all night.

away.en: at a distance from a particular place, or moving from a place

ex.en: The beach is two miles away.

baby.cue: 태어난 지 얼마 안 된 아이
baby.en: a very young child who has not yet begun to walk or talk

ex.en: The baby is sleeping in the crib.

back.en: the part of the body from the neck to the bottom of the spine

ex.en: My back hurts after sitting all day.
ex.ko: 하루 종일 앉아 있었더니 등이 아프다.

back.en: in or to a previous place or condition

ex.en: Please come back soon.

back.en: located at or near the rear of something

ex.en: The back door is locked.

back.en: support someone or something, especially with money

ex.en: The bank agreed to back the new project.
ex.ko: 은행은 그 새 프로젝트를 후원하기로 했다.

bad.en: not good; unpleasant or of low quality

ex.en: The weather was really bad yesterday.

bag.en: a container made of cloth, plastic, or paper, used for carrying things

ex.en: She put her books in a bag.

ball.en: a round object used in games and sports

ex.en: The kids are playing with a ball.

ball.en: a large formal party where people dance

ex.en: They danced all night at the ball.

banana.en: a long curved yellow fruit with soft sweet flesh

ex.en: He eats a banana every morning.

band.en: a group of musicians who play music together

ex.en: He plays guitar in a rock band.

band.en: a thin strip of material used to hold or decorate something

ex.en: She wore a hair band.

band.en: a group of people who share a common purpose

ex.en: A small band of rebels attacked the camp.

bar.en: a place where you can buy and drink alcoholic drinks

ex.en: We had a drink at the bar.
ex.ko: 우리는 바에서 술을 한 잔 마셨다.

bar.en: a long, straight piece of solid material such as metal or wood

ex.en: The window had metal bars.

bar.en: a small block of a solid substance such as chocolate or soap

ex.en: She bought a chocolate bar.

bar.en: prevent someone from entering a place or doing something

ex.en: He was barred from the club.

baseball.en: a sport played with a bat and ball between two teams of nine players

ex.en: They watch a baseball game every weekend.

basketball.en: a sport in which two teams try to score by throwing a ball through a high hoop

ex.en: He plays basketball after school.

bath.en: an act of washing your whole body while sitting in water

ex.en: She takes a bath every evening.

bathroom.en: a room with a bathtub or shower, and usually a toilet

ex.en: The bathroom is upstairs.

be.en: used to say what someone or something is or is like

ex.en: She is a doctor.

be.en: exist or be present in a place

ex.en: There is a book on the table.

beach.en: an area of sand or stones next to the sea

ex.en: We spent the day at the beach.

bean.en: the seed or pod of certain plants, eaten as food

ex.en: She cooked some green beans for dinner.

beautiful.en: very pleasant to look at or listen to

ex.en: She has a beautiful voice.
I stayed home because I was sick.

become.en: start to be something, or change into something

ex.en: She became a doctor last year.

bed.en: a piece of furniture that you sleep on

ex.en: He went to bed early last night.
My bedroom is on the second floor.

bee.en: a flying insect that makes honey and can sting

ex.en: A bee stung him on the arm.
We had beef for dinner.

before.en: earlier than a particular time or event

ex.en: Wash your hands before dinner.

begin.en: start to do something or start to happen

ex.en: The movie begins at seven.

behind.en: at or to the back of something

ex.en: The cat is hiding behind the sofa.

behind.en: the part of your body that you sit on (informal)

ex.en: He fell right on his behind.

believe.en: think that something is true or that someone is telling the truth

ex.en: I believe you.

bell.en: a hollow metal object that makes a ringing sound when struck, or a device that makes a similar sound

ex.en: The school bell rang loudly.

below.en: in or to a lower position than something

ex.en: The fish swam below the boat.

beside.en: next to or at the side of something

ex.en: She sat beside her friend.

best.en: of the highest quality or the most excellent (superlative of 'good')

ex.en: This is the best pizza I've ever had.
ex.ko: 이건 내가 먹어본 것 중 최고의 피자야.

better.en: of higher quality or more suitable (comparative of 'good')

ex.en: This new phone is better than my old one.
ex.ko: 이 새 휴대폰이 예전 것보다 낫다.

between.en: in the space or time separating two things or people

ex.en: The cat sat between the two chairs.

bicycle.en: a vehicle with two wheels that you ride by pushing pedals

ex.en: He rides his bicycle to school.

big.en: large in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: They live in a big house.
I ride my bike every day.
He bought a new bike last month.

bird.en: an animal with feathers and wings that can usually fly

ex.en: A small bird sang outside my window.

birth.en: the time when a baby is born

ex.en: She gave birth to a healthy baby.

birthday.en: the anniversary of the day someone was born

ex.en: Happy birthday to you!

biscuit.en: a small, flat, crisp baked snack

ex.en: She had tea and biscuits.

black.en: the darkest color, like the color of night

ex.en: Black is my favorite color.

black.en: having the darkest color, like the color of night

ex.en: He has a black car.

blank.en: having nothing written, printed, or recorded on it

ex.en: Please fill in the blank space.

blank.en: an empty space on a page or form to be filled in

ex.en: Write your name in the blank.

block.en: stop something from moving or passing through

ex.en: A fallen tree blocked the road.

block.en: an area of a city with streets on every side

ex.en: The store is two blocks away.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 두 블록 떨어져 있다.

block.en: a solid piece of a hard material, such as wood or stone, usually with flat sides

ex.en: The kids built a tower with wooden blocks.

blow.en: move air out of your mouth, or (of wind) move quickly

ex.en: The wind blew hard all night.

blow.en: a hard hit with a fist or object

ex.en: He took a heavy blow to the head.

blue.en: the color of a clear sky

ex.en: Blue is a calming color.

blue.en: having the color of a clear sky

ex.en: She has blue eyes.

board.en: get on a bus, plane, ship, or train

ex.en: Passengers can now board the plane.
ex.ko: 승객들은 이제 비행기에 탑승할 수 있다.

board.en: a flat, thin piece of wood or other hard material

ex.en: He nailed a board over the broken window.
ex.ko: 그는 깨진 창문에 판자를 못으로 박았다.

board.en: a group of people who officially manage or advise an organization

ex.en: The board approved the new budget.

boat.en: a small vehicle that travels on water

ex.en: They sailed the boat across the lake.

body.en: the whole physical structure of a person or animal

ex.en: Exercise is good for your body.

body.en: the physical body of a dead person or animal

ex.en: The police found a body in the river.

bone.en: one of the hard parts inside the body that form the skeleton

ex.en: He broke a bone in his leg.
I bought this novel at a bookstore.
The lecture was really boring.

borrow.en: take and use something that belongs to someone else, planning to return it

ex.en: Can I borrow your pen?

both.cue: 둘 중 하나가 아니라 둘 모두
both.en: the two things or people mentioned, not just one

ex.en: Both of my parents are teachers.
ex.ko: 우리 부모님은 두 분 다 선생님이시다.

bottle.en: a glass or plastic container for keeping liquids

ex.en: She drank a bottle of water.
The wine is bottled at the vineyard.
The keys are at the bottom of my bag.
ex.ko: 열쇠는 내 가방 맨 아래에 있다.

bowl.en: a round, deep dish used for holding food or liquid

ex.en: She filled the bowl with rice.

bowl.en: play the game of bowling by rolling a ball

ex.en: We went bowling on Friday night.
ex.ko: 우리는 금요일 밤에 볼링을 치러 갔다.

box.en: a container, usually rectangular, used for holding or carrying things

ex.en: He put the toys in a box.

box.en: fight using your fists as a sport

ex.en: He has been boxing for five years.
The boy is playing in the yard.

boyfriend.en: a man that someone is romantically involved with

ex.en: She introduced her boyfriend to her parents.

brain.en: the organ inside your head that controls thought, memory, and feeling

ex.en: The brain controls the whole body.

bread.en: food made from flour, water, and yeast, baked in an oven

ex.en: She bought a loaf of bread.

breakfast.en: the first meal of the day, eaten in the morning

ex.en: I usually have toast for breakfast.

breathe.en: take air into your lungs and let it out

ex.en: Take a deep breath and breathe slowly.

bridge.en: a structure built over a river or road so people can cross it

ex.en: We crossed the bridge to get to the city.
ex.ko: 우리는 도시로 가기 위해 다리를 건넜다.

ex.en: It's a bright sunny day.
She is a bright student.

bring.en: take something or someone with you to a place

ex.en: Please bring your umbrella tomorrow.

broken.en: damaged and no longer able to work or be whole

ex.en: My phone screen is broken.

brother.en: a boy or man who has the same parents as you

ex.en: I have one older brother.

brown.en: having the color of wood or coffee

ex.en: She has brown hair.

brown.en: the color of wood or coffee

ex.en: He painted the fence brown.

brush.en: a tool with bristles used for cleaning, painting, or styling hair

ex.en: She combed her hair with a brush.

brush.en: clean or smooth something using a brush

ex.en: Remember to brush your teeth twice a day.
ex.ko: 하루에 두 번 이를 닦는 것을 잊지 마.

bucket.en: a round container with a handle, used for carrying liquids

ex.en: He filled the bucket with water.

build.en: make something by putting materials and parts together

ex.en: They are building a new school.

building.en: a structure with a roof and walls, such as a house or school

ex.en: That tall building is a hospital.

burger.cue: 빵 사이에 고기 패티를 넣은 음식
burger.en: a sandwich made with a round bun and a cooked meat patty

ex.en: He ordered a burger and fries.

bus.en: a large vehicle that carries many passengers

ex.en: I take the bus to work every day.

business.en: the activity of buying and selling goods or services to make money

ex.en: She started her own business last year.

business.en: a matter that concerns a particular person

ex.en: It's none of your business.

busy.en: having a lot of things to do

ex.en: I'm very busy this week.

but.en: used to introduce something that contrasts with what was just said

ex.en: I like coffee, but I don't like tea.
ex.ko: 나는 커피는 좋아하지만 차는 좋아하지 않는다.

butter.en: a soft yellow food made from cream, spread on bread or used in cooking

ex.en: She spread butter on the toast.

butterfly.en: an insect with large, often colorful wings

ex.en: A butterfly landed on the flower.

button.en: a small round object used to fasten clothes

ex.en: One of my shirt buttons fell off.

button.en: a small object that you press to make a machine work

ex.en: Press the red button to start.

buy.en: get something by paying money for it

ex.en: I need to buy some milk.
She stood by the window.

by.en: used to show who or what does something

ex.en: The book was written by a famous author.
ex.ko: 그 책은 유명한 작가에 의해 쓰였다.

bye.en: used to say goodbye to someone

ex.en: Bye! See you tomorrow.

cafe.en: a small restaurant that sells coffee, drinks, and light meals

ex.en: Let's meet at the cafe near the station.

cake.en: a sweet food made from flour, eggs, and sugar, baked in an oven

ex.en: She baked a birthday cake.

call.en: give a name to someone or something, or say someone's name to get their attention

ex.en: They called the baby Sam.
I'll call you tomorrow.

call.en: an act of speaking to someone by telephone

ex.en: I got a call from my mom.

camera.en: a device used to take photographs or record video

ex.en: She took a photo with her camera.

camp.en: stay outdoors in a tent for a period of time

ex.en: We camped by the lake last summer.

camp.en: a place where people stay in tents or cabins, especially for a vacation or program

ex.en: The kids went to summer camp.

can.en: a metal container for food or drinks

ex.en: She opened a can of soda.

can.en: used to say that someone is able to do something

ex.en: I can swim very well.
ex.ko: 나는 수영을 아주 잘할 수 있다.

candy.en: a sweet food made mostly of sugar

ex.en: The kids love candy.

cap.en: a soft hat, often with a curved part at the front

ex.en: He wore a baseball cap.

cap.en: a cover for the top of a bottle or container

ex.en: Put the cap back on the bottle.

car.en: a road vehicle with four wheels, powered by an engine

ex.en: He drives his car to work.

card.en: a small piece of stiff paper with a message or picture, sent to someone

ex.en: She sent me a birthday card.

card.en: one of a set of pieces of stiff paper used for playing games

ex.en: They played a game of cards.

card.en: a small plastic card used to pay for things or prove your identity

ex.en: I paid with my credit card.

care.en: look after someone and make sure they are safe and well

ex.en: She cares for her elderly mother.

care.en: feel interested in or worried about something

ex.en: I really care about the environment.

care.en: the process of looking after someone

ex.en: The baby needs constant care.

careful.en: paying close attention to avoid mistakes or danger

ex.en: Be careful when crossing the street.

carefully.en: in a way that pays close attention to avoid mistakes or danger

ex.en: She carefully wrapped the gift.

april.en: the fourth month of the year

ex.en: My birthday is in April.

august.en: the eighth month of the year

ex.en: It's very hot in August.
